config.metrics.cpu: Enable CPU usage monitoring (percentage of total capacity)
config.metrics.memoryActive: Enable active memory monitoring (excluding buffers/cache, lower than used)
config.metrics.memoryUsed: Enable total used memory monitoring (including buffers/cache). Disabled by default since active memory is usually sufficient
config.metrics.network: Enable network upload/download speed monitoring (bytes per second)
config.metrics.fileSystem: Enable disk read/write speed monitoring (bytes per second, aggregated across all block devices)
config.metrics.battery: Enable battery level and charging status monitoring
config.metrics.cpuTemp: Enable CPU temperature monitoring (requires compatible sensors)
config.metrics.cpuSpeed: Enable current CPU clock speed monitoring (in GHz)
config.metrics.osDistro: Show operating system distribution name in the status bar. Disabled by default to keep the status bar compact
config.metrics.diskSpace: Enable disk space usage monitoring (usage percentage + used/total per mount point)
config.metrics.uptime: Enable system uptime display (days, hours, minutes since boot)
config.metricsOrder: Order of metric items in the status bar
config.refresh-interval: Data refresh interval in milliseconds (minimum 500ms, maximum 30000ms)
config.unitSystem: Unit system for file sizes and data rates. Binary (KiB/MiB/GiB, based on 1024) or Decimal (KB/MB/GB, based on 1000)
config.showSpace: Add a space between the numeric value and the unit label (e.g. "12.34 MiB" instead of "12.34MiB")
config.singleUnit: Show only the first letter of unit suffixes (e.g. "12.3K" instead of "12.3KiB", "1.5M" instead of "1.5MiB"). Works regardless of unitSystem setting
config.significantDigits: Number of significant digits per metric (e.g. 3 → "12.3 MiB", 4 → "12.34 MiB")
config.uptimeFormat: Custom format for the uptime display. Use placeholders: {d}=days, {h}=hours, {m}=minutes, {s}=seconds. Default "auto" shows "{d}d {h}h {m}m"
config.diskSpace: Disk space mount points to display ("all" shows every mounted filesystem; default shows only root / and C: to reduce space usage; add specific mount paths to filter)
config.notifications.enabled: Enable or disable all threshold notifications. When disabled, no notifications will be shown for any metric
config.notifications.cpu: CPU usage notification threshold (%). Set to 0 to disable this notification
config.notifications.memoryActive: Active memory usage notification threshold (%). Set to 0 to disable this notification
config.notifications.memoryUsed: Total memory usage notification threshold (%). Set to 0 to disable this notification
config.notifications.cpuTemp: CPU temperature notification threshold (°C). Set to 0 to disable this notification
config.notifications.batteryLow: Low battery notification threshold (%). Triggers when battery drops to or below this level and the device is on battery power. Set to 0 to disable
config.notifications.batteryHigh: High battery notification threshold (%). Triggers when battery charges to or above this level and the device is plugged in. Set to 0 to disable
metric.battery.name: Battery Status
metric.cpu.name: CPU Usage
metric.cpuSpeed.name: CPU Speed
metric.cpuTemp.name: CPU Temperature
metric.diskSpace.name: Disk Space
metric.fileSystem.name: File System Usage (Write/Read)
metric.memoryActive.name: Memory Active (excl. buffers/cache)
metric.memoryUsed.name: Memory Used (incl. buffers/cache)
metric.network.name: Network Usage (Down/Up)
metric.osDistro.name: OS Distribution
metric.uptime.name: System running time
viewContainer.monitor-pro.title: Monitor Pro
config.resourceUsage.charts: Per-chart settings: enabled state and default view mode (line/bar). Keys: cpu, memActive, memUsed, netRx, netTx, diskRx, diskWx, diskSpace
config.resourceUsage.showBattery: Show battery level and charging status in the Resource Usage view
config.resourceUsage.showCpuTemp: Show CPU temperature in the Resource Usage view
config.resourceUsage.showCpuSpeed: Show CPU clock speed in the Resource Usage view
config.resourceUsage.diskSpaceMounts: Mount points to display for the Disk Space chart. Use ["all"] to show every mounted filesystem, or add specific mount paths to filter
config.resourceUsage.samplingPoints: Number of sampling points to display on the chart. Increase for a longer history view, decrease for better performance
config.resourceUsage.showUptime: Show system uptime in the Resource Usage view info section
config.resourceUsage.showOsDistro: Show OS distribution name in the Resource Usage view info section
